name: Failnaught
description: Failnaught, also known as the 'Sure Spear' or 'Spear of Destiny' in some traditions, is a legendary spear associated with the Arthurian cycle, particularly with Sir Lancelot. While not as prominently featured as Excalibur, Failnaught is a weapon of immense power and significance. Its origins are often shrouded in mystery, with some accounts suggesting it was forged by otherworldly beings or bestowed upon Lancelot as a reward for his valor and chivalry. The spear is said to possess unparalleled accuracy and force, never missing its target and delivering devastating blows. It embodies Lancelot's skill and prowess as a warrior, representing his ability to overcome any obstacle. The spear's name, Failnaught, literally translates to 'no failure' or 'sure shot,' highlighting its unerring precision. Its presence in Arthurian tales underscores the themes of courage, destiny, and the extraordinary capabilities of the knights of the Round Table. Some legends claim the spear was imbued with magic, granting it further powers beyond its physical attributes. Although not as widely recognized as other Arthurian artifacts, Failnaught contributes to the rich tapestry of the Arthurian world, adding depth to Lancelot's character and the overall narrative of the kingdom's rise and fall. The spear symbolizes Lancelot’s unmatched combat abilities and serves as a reminder of his pivotal role in the Arthurian saga. It signifies that even in the face of overwhelming odds, true skill and unwavering resolve can lead to victory. Failnaught is more than just a weapon; it is a testament to the ideals and values of the Arthurian knights.
subtitle: The Sure Spear of Sir Lancelot
origin: Forged by otherworldly beings, or given as reward

keyMyths.title: Lancelot's Triumph
keyMyths.description: Failnaught ensures Lancelot's victory in numerous battles, demonstrating its unerring accuracy and power. It helps establish his reputation as the greatest knight.

keyMyths.title: The Lost Spear
keyMyths.description: The fate of Failnaught after the fall of Camelot is unknown, adding to its mystique. It is speculated to be lost to the ages, awaiting a worthy wielder.

sources.author: Sir Thomas Malory
sources.description: Provides foundational stories of Lancelot and his prowess.
sources.text: Provides foundational stories of Lancelot and his prowess.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Lancelot, the Knight of the Cart
sources.description: Details Lancelot's heroic deeds and equipment.
